heading: Delineation of wetlands; exemptions
label: 3822 Delineation of wetlands; exemptions

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Subject to subsection (b) and paragraph (6), the Secretary shall delineate, determine, and certify all wetlands located on subject land on a farm.

children.heading: Wetland delineation maps
children.label: 2 Wetland delineation maps
children.text: The Secretary shall delineate wetlands on wetland delineation maps. On the request of a person, the Secretary shall make a reasonable effort to make an on-site wetland determination prior to delineation.

children.label: A
children.text: certify whether a map is sufficient for the purpose of making a determination of ineligibility for program benefits under ; andsection 3821 of this title

children.label: B
children.text: provide an opportunity to appeal the certification prior to the certification becoming final.

children.heading: Duration of certification
children.label: 4 Duration of certification
children.text: A final certification made under paragraph (3) shall remain valid and in effect as long as the area is devoted to an agricultural use or until such time as the person affected by the certification requests review of the certification by the Secretary.

children.heading: Review of mapping on appeal
children.label: 5 Review of mapping on appeal
children.text: In the case of an appeal of the Secretary’s certification, the Secretary shall review and certify the accuracy of the mapping of all land subject to the appeal to ensure that the subject land has been accurately delineated. Prior to rendering a decision on the appeal, the Secretary shall conduct an on-site inspection of the subject land on a farm.

children.heading: Reliance on prior certified delineation
children.label: 6 Reliance on prior certified delineation
children.text: No person shall be adversely affected because of having taken an action based on a previous certified wetland delineation by the Secretary. The delineation shall not be subject to a subsequent wetland certification or delineation by the Secretary, unless requested by the person under paragraph (4).

children.label: A
children.text: A converted wetland if the conversion of the wetland was commenced before .December 23, 1985

children.label: B
children.text: Land that is a nontidal drainage or irrigation ditch excavated in upland.

children.label: C
children.text: A wet area created by a water delivery system, irrigation, irrigation system, or application of water for irrigation.

children.label: D
children.text: A wetland on which the owner or operator of a farm or ranch uses normal cropping or ranching practices to produce an agricultural commodity in a manner that is consistent for the area where the production is possible as a result of a natural condition, such as drought, and is without action by the producer that destroys a natural wetland characteristic.

children.label: E
children.text: Land that is an artificial lake or pond created by excavating or diking land (that is not a wetland) to collect and retain water and that is used primarily for livestock watering, fish production, irrigation, wildlife, fire control, flood control, cranberry growing, or rice production, or as a settling pond.

children.label: F
children.text: A wetland that is temporarily or incidentally created as a result of adjacent development activity.

children.label: i
children.text: the lack of maintenance of drainage, dikes, levees, or similar structures;

children.label: ii
children.text: a lack of management of the lands containing the wetland; or

children.label: iii
children.text: circumstances beyond the control of the person.

children.label: i
children.text: the converted wetland was determined by the Natural Resources Conservation Service to have been manipulated for the production of an agricultural commodity or forage prior to , and was returned to wetland conditions through a voluntary restoration, enhancement, or creation action subsequent to that determination;December 23, 1985

children.label: ii
children.text: technical determinations regarding the prior site conditions and the restoration, enhancement, or creation action have been adequately documented by the Natural Resources Conservation Service;

children.label: iii
children.text: the proposed conversion action is approved by the Natural Resources Conservation Service prior to implementation; and

children.label: iv
children.text: the extent of the proposed conversion is limited so that the conditions will be at least equivalent to the wetland functions and values that existed prior to implementation of the voluntary wetland restoration, enhancement, or creation action.

children.label: A
children.text: An artificial lake or pond created by excavating or diking land that is not a wetland to collect and retain water and that is used primarily for livestock watering, fish production, irrigation, wildlife, fire control, flood control, cranberry growing, rice production, or as a settling pond.

children.label: B
children.text: A wetland that is temporarily or incidentally created as a result of adjacent development activity.

children.label: C
children.text: A wetland on which the owner or operator of a farm or ranch uses normal cropping or ranching practices to produce an agricultural commodity in a manner that is consistent for the area where the production is possible as a result of a natural condition, such as drought, and is without action by the producer that destroys a natural wetland characteristic.

children.label: i
children.text: the lack of maintenance of drainage, dikes, levees, or similar structures;

children.label: ii
children.text: a lack of management of the lands containing the wetland; or

children.label: iii
children.text: circumstances beyond the control of the person.

children.label: i
children.text: the wetland was determined by the Natural Resources Conservation Service to have been manipulated for the production of an agricultural commodity or forage prior to , and was returned to wetland conditions through a voluntary restoration, enhancement, or creation action subsequent to that determination;December 23, 1985

children.label: ii
children.text: technical determinations regarding the prior site conditions and the restoration, enhancement, or creation action have been adequately documented by the Natural Resources Conservation Service;

children.label: iii
children.text: the proposed conversion action is approved by the Natural Resources Conservation Service prior to implementation; and

children.label: iv
children.text: the extent of the proposed conversion is limited so that the conditions will be at least equivalent to the wetland functions and values that existed prior to implementation of the voluntary wetland restoration, enhancement, or creation action.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: No program loans, payments, or benefits shall be withheld from a person under this subchapter unless the Secretary has conducted an on-site visit of the subject land, which, except as provided in paragraph (2), shall be conducted in the presence of the affected person.

children.heading: Exception
children.label: 2 Exception
children.text: The Secretary may conduct an on-site visit under paragraph (1) without the affected person present if the Secretary has made a reasonable effort to include the presence of the affected person at the on-site visit.

children.heading: Identification of minimal effect exemptions
children.label: d Identification of minimal effect exemptions
children.text: For purposes of applying the minimal effect exemption under subsection (f)(1), the Secretary shall identify by regulation categorical minimal effect exemptions on a regional basis to assist persons in avoiding a violation of the ineligibility provisions of . The Secretary shall ensure that employees of the Department of Agriculture who administer this subchapter receive appropriate training to properly apply the minimal effect exemptions determined by the Secretary.section 3821 of this title
Such lands have a predominance of hydric soils.

Such lands are inundated or saturated by surface or groundwater at a frequency and duration sufficient to support a prevalence of hydrophytic vegetation typically adapted for life in saturated soil conditions.
Such lands, under normal circumstances, support a prevalence of such vegetation.

The action, individually and in connection with all other similar actions authorized by the Secretary in the area, will have a minimal effect on the functional hydrological and biological value of the wetlands in the area, including the value to waterfowl and wildlife.

children.label: A
children.text: in accordance with a wetland conservation plan;

children.label: B
children.text: in advance of, or concurrent with, the action;

children.label: C
children.text: not at the expense of the Federal Government;

children.label: D
children.text: in the case of enhancement or restoration of wetlands, on not greater than a 1-for-1 acreage basis unless more acreage is needed to provide equivalent functions and values that will be lost as a result of the wetland conversion to be mitigated;

children.label: E
children.text: in the case of creation of wetlands, on greater than a 1-for-1 acreage basis if more acreage is needed to provide equivalent functions and values that will be lost as a result of the wetland conversion that is mitigated;

children.label: F
children.text: on lands in the same general area of the local watershed as the converted wetland; and

children.label: i
children.text: is recorded on public land records;

children.label: ii
children.text: remains in force for as long as the converted wetland for which the restoration, enhancement, or creation to be mitigated remains in agricultural use or is not returned to its original wetland classification with equivalent functions and values; and

children.label: iii
children.text: prohibits making alterations to the restored, enhanced, or created wetland that lower the wetland’s functions and values.

The wetland was converted after , but before , and the wetland values, acreage, and functions are mitigated by the producer through the requirements of subparagraphs (A), (B), (C), (D), (F), and (G) of paragraph (2).December 23, 1985November 28, 1990

The action was authorized by a permit issued under and the wetland values, acreage, and functions of the converted wetland were adequately mitigated for the purposes of this subchapter.section 1344 of title 33

children.heading: Mitigation appeals
children.label: g Mitigation appeals
children.text: A person shall be afforded the right to appeal, under section 3843 of this title, the imposition of a mitigation agreement requiring greater than one-to-one acreage mitigation to which the person is subject.1See References in Text note below.1

children.heading: Exemption described
children.label: 1 Exemption described
children.text: The Secretary may waive a person’s ineligibility under for program loans, payments, and benefits as the result of the conversion of a wetland subsequent to , or the production of an agricultural commodity on a converted wetland, if the Secretary determines that the person has acted in good faith and without intent to violate this subchapter.section 3821 of this titleNovember 28, 1990

children.label: A
children.text: State Executive Director, with the technical concurrence of the State Conservationist; or

children.label: B
children.text: district director, with the technical concurrence of the area conservationist.

children.heading: Period for compliance
children.label: 3 Period for compliance
children.text: The Secretary shall provide a person who the Secretary determines has acted in good faith and without intent to violate this subchapter with a reasonable period, but not to exceed 1 year, during which to implement the measures and practices necessary to be considered to be actively restoring the subject wetland.

children.heading: Restoration
children.label: i Restoration
children.text: Any person who is determined to be ineligible for program benefits under for any crop year shall not be ineligible for such program benefits under such section for any subsequent crop year if, prior to the beginning of such subsequent crop year, the person has fully restored the characteristics of the converted wetland to its prior wetland state or has otherwise mitigated for the loss of wetland values, as determined by the Secretary, through the restoration, enhancement, or creation of wetland values in the same general area of the local watershed as the converted wetland.section 3821 of this title

children.heading: Determinations; restoration and mitigation plans; monitoring activities
children.label: j Determinations; restoration and mitigation plans; monitoring activities
children.text: Technical determinations, the development of restoration and mitigation plans, and monitoring activities under this section shall be made by the Natural Resources Conservation Service.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Using authorities available to the Secretary, the Secretary shall operate a program or work with third parties to establish mitigation banks to assist persons in complying with the provisions of this section while mitigating any loss of wetland values and functions.

children.heading: Authorization of appropriations
children.label: B Authorization of appropriations
children.text: There is authorized to be appropriated to the Secretary to carry out this paragraph $5,000,000 for each of fiscal years 2019 through 2023.

children.heading: Applicability
children.label: 2 Applicability
children.text: Subsection (f)(2)(C) shall not apply to this subsection.

children.heading: Policy and criteria
children.label: 3 Policy and criteria
children.text: The Secretary shall develop the appropriate policy and criteria that will allow willing persons to access existing mitigation banks, under this section or any other authority, that will serve the purposes of this section without requiring the Secretary to hold an easement, in whole or in part, in a mitigation bank.
